message:
fix(stream,sync): repoll-after-completion guard for Next future and wake-outside-lock refactor for Notify

Next future (src/stream/next.rs):
- Add a `done` boolean field that latches true once poll returns Ready
- Assert `!done` at the top of poll() so callers who repoll after
  completion get a clear panic instead of silently re-driving the
  underlying stream, which could violate stream contracts
- Two new tests: repoll after Ready(Some) and repoll after Ready(None)
  both verify the panic fires via catch_unwind

Notify::notify_one (src/sync/notify.rs):
- Restructure to extract the waker *inside* the lock scope but call
  waker.wake() *after* dropping the lock, preventing user-supplied
  waker code from executing while the waiter state mutex is held
- This eliminates a potential deadlock vector if a Waker::wake()
  implementation re-enters Notify (e.g. to register another waiter)
- Remove the now-unnecessary clippy::significant_drop_tightening allow
  since the lock is explicitly scoped
- Minor: reorder std::sync imports to canonical alphabetical order

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
